notes: Model switch mid-session: two no-op turns separated by a model switch. antigravity writes a <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> block on each switch; the parser harvests the model and (via MergeMetrics latest-wins) ModelName should read model B by turn 2. KEY RISK record must settle: the v1 parser harvests the model ONLY while p.model=='' (parseUserInput), so a SECOND mid-session switch may NOT flip ModelName — if the recorded ModelName stays model A after the switch, this becomes daemon=bug (parser ignores later switches) and the meta must be re-marked known_failing. The spec here asserts the CORRECT behavior (ModelName flips A->B). model observations left PROVISIONAL for record to fill (both A and B). cost/tokens OMITTED (SQLite-only). driver=gap:wait_turn (+ possibly gap:keys if the switch is an arrow-key picker not an inline slash): record ports the turn-done poll + the switch primitive before driving.

text: Session appears in ready (first phase anchored UNPINNED to start)
Turn 1 user prompt -> working (model A active)
Turn 1 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = model A
The model switch (slash/picker) runs locally with no LLM round-trip; state stays ready, no working/waiting flicker
Turn 2 user prompt -> working (model B active; a second <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> recorded)
Turn 2 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = model B (latest-wins). NB: if the v1 parser's p.model=='' guard suppresses the second switch, record re-marks daemon=bug/known_failing.
